verses.text: Hearken to the statutes; add nothing, take nothing away. Hearken. God's word is to be kept whole.
Hearken to the statutes; add nothing, take nothing away. Hearken. God's word is to be kept whole.
Hearken to the statutes; add nothing, take nothing away. Hearken. God's word is to be kept whole.
Hearken to the statutes; add nothing, take nothing away. Hearken. God's word is to be kept whole.
No nation has statutes so righteous, nor a God so near. So near. Israel is privileged above all.
No nation has statutes so righteous, nor a God so near. So near. Israel is privileged above all.
No nation has statutes so righteous, nor a God so near. So near. Israel is privileged above all.
No nation has statutes so righteous, nor a God so near. So near. Israel is privileged above all.
Forget not what your eyes saw; teach it to your children. Remember. Pass on God's works.
Forget not what your eyes saw; teach it to your children. Remember. Pass on God's works.
At Horeb they heard God's voice but saw no form. No form. They heard, but saw no shape.
At Horeb they heard God's voice but saw no form. No form. They heard, but saw no shape.
At Horeb they heard God's voice but saw no form. No form. They heard, but saw no shape.
At Horeb they heard God's voice but saw no form. No form. They heard, but saw no shape.
Make no graven image, for they saw no similitude. No image. God is not to be pictured.
Make no graven image, for they saw no similitude. No image. God is not to be pictured.
Make no graven image, for they saw no similitude. No image. God is not to be pictured.
Make no graven image, for they saw no similitude. No image. God is not to be pictured.
Make no graven image, for they saw no similitude. No image. God is not to be pictured.
God took them for His own; 'the LORD thy God is a consuming fire.' Jealous. He will not share His glory.
God took them for His own; 'the LORD thy God is a consuming fire.' Jealous. He will not share His glory.
God took them for His own; 'the LORD thy God is a consuming fire.' Jealous. He will not share His glory.
God took them for His own; 'the LORD thy God is a consuming fire.' Jealous. He will not share His glory.
God took them for His own; 'the LORD thy God is a consuming fire.' Jealous. He will not share His glory.
If they corrupt themselves with idols, they shall be scattered. Warning. Idolatry brings exile.
If they corrupt themselves with idols, they shall be scattered. Warning. Idolatry brings exile.
If they corrupt themselves with idols, they shall be scattered. Warning. Idolatry brings exile.
If they corrupt themselves with idols, they shall be scattered. Warning. Idolatry brings exile.
Yet if they seek Him with all their heart, He will not forsake them. Mercy. God receives the returning.
Yet if they seek Him with all their heart, He will not forsake them. Mercy. God receives the returning.
Yet if they seek Him with all their heart, He will not forsake them. Mercy. God receives the returning.
No nation ever heard God speak and lived; 'the LORD he is God.' He is God. There is none else.
No nation ever heard God speak and lived; 'the LORD he is God.' He is God. There is none else.
No nation ever heard God speak and lived; 'the LORD he is God.' He is God. There is none else.
No nation ever heard God speak and lived; 'the LORD he is God.' He is God. There is none else.
He brought them out by His great power. His power. Redemption proves His love.
He brought them out by His great power. His power. Redemption proves His love.
He brought them out by His great power. His power. Redemption proves His love.
'Know therefore... the LORD he is God'; keep His commandments. Know it. Let it shape your life.
'Know therefore... the LORD he is God'; keep His commandments. Know it. Let it shape your life.
Moses sets apart three cities of refuge east of Jordan. Refuge. Mercy is provided.
Moses sets apart three cities of refuge east of Jordan. Refuge. Mercy is provided.
Moses sets apart three cities of refuge east of Jordan. Refuge. Mercy is provided.
This is the law Moses set before Israel, beyond Jordan. The law. The discourse is introduced.
This is the law Moses set before Israel, beyond Jordan. The law. The discourse is introduced.
This is the law Moses set before Israel, beyond Jordan. The law. The discourse is introduced.
They possessed the land of Sihon and Og east of Jordan. The land. The conquest is noted.
They possessed the land of Sihon and Og east of Jordan. The land. The conquest is noted.
